latest.title: Proposed Commercial Relationship
latest.content:
21 May 2026 07:05
RNS Number : 1669F
Haydale PLC
21 May 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 (AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018).
Haydale plc
("Haydale", the "Company" or the "Group")
Proposed Commercial Relationship
Response to Sabien Technology Group plc Announcement
Haydale (AIM: HAYD),
the advanced materials and clean-technology group, notes the announcement released this morning by Sabien Technology Group plc ("Sabien") regarding a proposed commercial distribution relationship involving SaveMoneyCutCarbon ("SMCC"), Haydale's embedded B2B go-to-market platform.
The Board is pleased to see further validation of the SMCC platform, its national deployment capability and broader commercial strategy.
Discussions between the parties remain ongoing and no definitive agreements have yet been entered into. Further announcements will be made in due course, should substantive commercial agreements be concluded.
ENDS
For further information
Haydale plc
Simon Turek, Chief Executive OfficerPatrick Carter, Chief Financial Officer
Mark Sait, Chief Commercial OfficerTel: +44 (0) 1269 842946
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Nominated Adviser & Broker)
Corporate Finance - Julian Blunt / Edward Whiley / Trisyia JamaludinCorporate Broking - Andrew BurdisTel: +44 (0) 20 7220 0500
About Haydale
Haydale plc is an advanced materials and clean-technology company focused on the development and deployment of energy- and water-efficient technologies at scale. The Group's mission is to improve the energy, water and carbon performance of one million buildings.
The Group leverages its proprietary HDPlas® platform technology to develop patented graphene-enabled products that deliver measurable energy, water and carbon savings. These products are deployed through an integrated commercial model combining product innovation, customer access, delivery capability and Impact Partner relationships.
SaveMoneyCutCarbon operates as the Group's embedded B2B go-to-market platform, providing a national sales, programme management and installer network capability. Through its Impact Partner Programme, SMCC originates pre-qualified demand via long-term agreements with UK banks and utilities.
Haydale's strategy is focused on scaling the deployment of cost-effective decarbonisation solutions across the built environment, supported by measurable outcomes and financing structures that enable adoption.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: New Contract Signed
latest.content:
7 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4265Z
Haydale PLC
07 April 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 (AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018).
Haydale plc
("Haydale", the "Company" or the "Group")
New Contract Signed
Wave partnership expands to exclusive multi-year framework with embedded national rollout
Haydale (AIM: HAYD)
, the advanced materials and clean-technology group, provides a commercial update on the expansion of its SaveMoneyCutCarbon ("SMCC") platform through a multi-year framework agreement and exclusivity arrangement with Wave Utilities ("Wave") (the "Agreement").
Wave is the largest business water retailer in England by water volume, serving over 300,000 business customers. It is a joint venture between Anglian Water and Northumbrian Water.
This Agreement reflects the continued growth of SMCC's water efficiency business unit, materially increasing the scale, visibility and repeatability of revenue generation within the SMCC platform.
Contract expansion and exclusivity
The existing partnership is anchored by a three-year schools programme, which commenced in 2025. Delivery to date has validated both the commercial and operational model.
Through the new Agreement, SMCC and Wave have now agreed terms to expand the partnership, with SMCC delivering water efficiency programmes across Wave's commercial and public sector customer base.
As part of this Agreement, SMCC has been appointed as Wave's exclusive external delivery partner for water efficiency audits and has secured right of first refusal on the delivery of funded water efficiency projects, embedding SMCC within Wave's national rollout.
The Agreement is expected to generate at least £1.0m of recurring programme-based revenue annually, with a broader pipeline of identified opportunities currently valued at approximately £5.7m over the medium term.
The programme is structured to drive high conversion from audit to deployment, underpinning revenue visibility and repeatable delivery across Wave's customer base.
In addition, the partnership provides SMCC with direct access to a large base of commercial and public sector sites, creating opportunities to deliver a broader range of energy-efficiency solutions, including JustHeat, renewables and EV infrastructure.
From Contract to Platform
The Wave Agreement is a representative example of SMCC's Impact Partner model, whereby long-term agreements with utilities and financial institutions provide embedded access to large, pre-qualified customer bases.
This model is repeatable across multiple partners, with each new agreement adding incremental customer access, programme volume and revenue visibility.
The Company believes that, as this model scales, it has the potential to support multi-partner programme deployment across tens of thousands of sites, creating a pathway to material revenue growth across the SMCC platform.
Strategic Importance
Water efficiency is an increasingly important component of the SMCC platform, reflecting its role in reducing both cost and carbon across commercial and public sector estates.
The expansion of the Wave partnership demonstrates the continued evolution of SMCC into a programme delivery platform for large-scale retrofit and resource efficiency initiatives.
These programmes are expected to convert into recurring, programme-based revenues as deployment scales.
The combination of Wave's large and established customer base, committed water reduction targets and programme-based delivery approach provides a strong foundation for sustained growth within the water-efficiency segment.
The Company expects this Agreement to contribute materially to the continued growth of the SMCC platform expected during FY26 and beyond. It also represents a continued shift towards recurring, multi-year revenue relationships underpinned by long-term customer engagement and programme delivery.
The Company believes this model is repeatable across additional partners and sectors, supporting its strategy of improving the energy, water and carbon performance of one million buildings.
Simon Turek, CEO of Haydale, commented:
"The expansion of our partnership with Wave demonstrates the SMCC platform scaling into multi-year, programme-led delivery with clear revenue visibility.
Wave provides both the scale and the structure to deploy solutions across thousands of sites in a repeatable way, with programmes extending beyond initial audit into ongoing project delivery and broader energy and carbon solutions.
This creates a clear pathway from customer access through to programme delivery and long-term revenue generation.
This is the model that drives repeatable revenue at scale."
Mark Sait, CEO of SaveMoneyCutCarbon, commented:
"The expansion of our relationship with Wave reflects the success of the model we have built.
We are now deploying water efficiency solutions at scale across multiple sectors, with clear pathways from initial engagement through to full programme rollout.
Water efficiency is often overlooked, yet it represents one of the most immediate and cost-effective ways for organisations to reduce both their environmental impact and operating costs.
By integrating water, energy and carbon solutions into a single offering, we are able to provide customers with a more joined-up and practical approach to improving the performance of their buildings.
Through the expanded Agreement, we are able to support more customers, across more sites, with practical solutions that deliver measurable savings while building long-term relationships that underpin recurring programme activity."
Oli Shelley, Director of Water Efficiency Services at Wave, commented:
"We are pleased to be extending our partnership with SaveMoneyCutCarbon as we scale delivery of the Big Blue Eco Booster project nationally.
Supporting customers to reduce water consumption is central to our long-term sustainability commitments. By working with SMCC, we are able to combine practical on-site delivery with expert insight, helping organisations to reduce waste, lower costs and contribute to broader environmental targets.
This expanded agreement enables us to accelerate that impact across a larger number of sites and customer groups."
For further information
Haydale plc
Simon Turek, Chief Executive OfficerPatrick Carter, Chief Financial Officer
Mark Sait, Chief Commercial OfficerTel: +44 (0) 1269 842946
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Nominated Adviser & Broker)
Corporate Finance - Julian Blunt / Edward Whiley / Trisyia JamaludinCorporate Broking - Andrew BurdisTel: +44 (0) 20 7220 0500

The Group leverages its proprietary HDPlas® platform technology to develop patented graphene-enabled products that deliver measurable energy, water and carbon savings. These products are deployed through an integrated commercial model combining product innovation, customer access, delivery capability and Impact Partner relationships.
SaveMoneyCutCarbon operates as the Group's embedded B2B go-to-market platform, providing a national sales, programme management and installer network capability. Through its Impact Partner Programme, SMCC originates pre-qualified demand via long-term agreements with UK banks and utilities.

About Haydale
Haydale plc is an advanced materials and clean-technology company focused on the development and deployment of energy- and water-efficient technologies at scale.
The Group leverages its proprietary HDPlas® platform technology to develop patented graphene-enabled products that deliver measurable energy, water and carbon savings. These products are deployed through an integrated commercial model that combines in-house product development with established customer access, delivery capability and Impact Partner relationships.
SaveMoneyCutCarbon is the Group's embedded B2B go-to-market platform, operating a national sales, programme management and installer network. Through its Impact Partner Programme, SMCC originates pre-qualified SME and corporate demand via long-term agreements with UK banks and utilities. This partner-funded acquisition model provides scalable, low-cost customer origination and recurring programme revenue.
Haydale's strategy is focused on accelerating the deployment of cost-effective decarbonisation solutions across the UK's built environment, while building a scalable platform for wider international deployment through strategic partners. The Group is execution-led and product-focused, translating advanced materials science into commercially deployable products and solutions that deliver measurable energy, water and carbon savings.

the advanced materials and clean-technology group
, today announces that Simon Turek, Chief Executive Officer, and Patrick Carter, Chief Financial Officer, will provide a live presentation relating to the Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company on 11 March 2026, at 12:00 p.m.

company_30s.what_they_do: HAYD.L supplies digital infrastructure services—think cloud‑connected networks and data‑center solutions—to businesses and public‑sector clients in the UK.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The push to Netzero and the digital‑first agenda are creating a huge, captive market for reliable connectivity. Companies that can deliver fast, secure, and scalable infrastructure are positioned to win new contracts and lock in long‑term revenue.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a growth phase: it has just signed several commercial deals, announced a strategic partnership, and completed a small equity raise. Cash burn is present but it’s moving from a survival mode to a scaling mode, and the stock sits well below its 52‑week high.

rns_narrative: The first theme is a wave of new contracts and commercial expansion—multiple RNS items highlight wins and a broadened platform that will serve more clients.
The second theme is partnership and infrastructure: the company is aligning with larger players to strengthen its offering and gain market credibility.
The third theme is capital activity: directors have been active in share dealings, and a recent equity issuance suggests the company is securing fresh cash to fund growth.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Captive market opportunity from the Netzero push
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the Netzero mandate as a guaranteed source of demand, noting that the company is building a niche in a growing market.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: There are no overt bearish posts, but the lack of revenue detail and heavy share‑deal activity hint that some investors fear the company may over‑value itself before the contracts pay off.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from neutral to mildly bullish as recent contract wins surface.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat—there’s no public chatter, which gives us a surprise edge when a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a new multi‑year contract in the next 30 days could ignite buying.
The next quarterly earnings release, which is expected to show a revenue uptick, will be a clear signal.
If the company closes its recent equity round or raises a new tranche, the influx of capital could reduce cash‑burn risk and lift confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm a bounce are: 1) a 20‑day moving‑average breakout accompanied by volume more than 1.5× the 3‑month average; 2) a price break of the 10‑day support level with a 5‑minute spike in volume; 3) a public statement of a new partnership or contract win.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score indicates a tight price compression, suggesting we’re in the early stages of a mean‑reversion cycle. Capitulation appears finished; the next phase will be a gradual climb once volume and a catalyst trigger.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen 86% from its peak—a classic deep‑crash scenario that historically delivers large rebounds. If the price recovers to just half its pre‑crash level, that’s roughly a 70% upside. The recent contract wins and Netzero relevance give a strong fundamental driver for a rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main downside risk is that the company may not convert its contracts into cash flow quickly enough, and continued cash burn could force a further decline. A realistic worst‑case would be a 10‑12% drop from the current price if the market doubts the company’s ability to monetize its pipeline.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 10‑12% of the entry price. Potential gain: ~70% upside. That’s a 1:7 asymmetry—an attractive trade for a contrarian.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a volume spike that follows a contract announcement or earnings release. Use a 5% stop‑loss below the 10‑day support level. Position size: 10‑15% of discretionary capital to stay within risk limits.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a breakout above the 20‑day moving average with volume >1.5× the 3‑month average. That confirms the mean‑reversion thesis and reduces the probability of a false break.
playbook.if_passing: There are no immediate terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy. The only red flag would be a sustained decline that pushes the price into a 20‑day support breach with no accompanying catalyst.

ai_insights.bottom_line: HAYD.L sits at a deep crash, but the Netzero‑driven demand and recent contract wins set up a high‑reward mean‑reversion play with a strong asymmetry.
